summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships persistent cryptocurrency clipboard hijacker
details: Package name `@jaggle/resizeobserves` is a typosquat of `@juggle/resize-observer` and reuses that package's README verbatim, but ships no ResizeObserver implementation. The npm `postinstall` script (`npm-install.cjs`) installs python3-pip via the OS package manager (apt/pacman/dnf, falling back to fetching get-pip.py), pip-installs a bundled `clipboard_guardian` Python package using `--break-system-packages`, and establishes persistence under deceptive OS-mimicking names: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` on Linux (`ExecStart=...Restart=always`), a LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist` on macOS, and an HKCU Run key `PyRuntimeBroker` on Windows. The installed daemon (`clipboard_guardian/guardian.py`) polls the clipboard, detects cryptocurrency addresses across 35+ chains (BTC/ETH/SOL/XMR/LTC/TRX/...), and rewrites them to attacker-controlled wallets hardcoded in the source (e.g., Ethereum `0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18`) before writing them back to the clipboard — so any address the user later pastes into a wallet UI is silently substituted with the attacker's. The daemon additionally renames its process via `setproctitle` to match its disguised service name and enumerates running process monitors (taskmgr.exe, procexp.exe, processhacker.exe, htop, btop, gnome-system-monitor) for evasion. Typosquat name + plagiarized README + clipboard hijacker + multi-OS persistence under OS-impersonating names + anti-forensics all establish unambiguous installer-targeting malware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
